diffs.patch:
Index: bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md
===================================================================
--- bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md	
+++ bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md	
@@ -0,0 +1,165 @@
+# Routing Read/Write Ledger
+
+## src/hono.ts
+- Purpose: Main Hono entry point that creates the Hono class extending HonoBase with default SmartRouter configuration
+- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, RegExpRouter, SmartRouter, SmartRouter, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema
+- Simple note: Constructor creates SmartRouter with [RegExpRouter, TrieRouter](src/hono.ts:31) unless custom router is provided
+
+## src/hono-base.ts
+- Purpose: Base class for Hono framework with router, context, request dispatching, handler registration
+- Names noticed: HonoBase, Hono, HonoOptions, Router, H, HandlerInterface, Context, MiddlewareHandler, Next, compose, getPath, getPathNoStrict, mergePath, METHODS, METHOD_NAME_ALL, METHOD_NAME_ALL_LOWERCASE, Request, Response, ExecutionContext, FetchEventLike, RouterRoute, Error
+- Simple note: Provides `constructor` with route registration, `#dispatch` for request handling, `fetch`/`request` as entry points, handles middleware composition via `#addRoute`
+
+## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module export for PatternRouter (index file)
+- Names noticed: PatternRouter
+- Simple note: Simple re-export from 'router'
+
+## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: PatternRouter implements router interface, builds regex patterns from path definitions at runtime
+- Names noticed: PatternRouter, add, match, Route, REGEXP_MATCH, UnsupportedPathError, METHOD_NAME_ALL, emptyParams
+- Simple note: Adds converted paths with regex and matches on request, throws UnsupportedPathError for duplicate param names
+
+## src/router/linear-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module export for LinearRouter (index file)
+- Names noticed: LinearRouter
+- Simple note: Simple re-export from 'router'
+
+## src/router/linear-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: LinearRouter implements linear path matching, adds routes to array, matches sequentially for fastest path
+- Names noticed: LinearRouter, add, match, ROUTES_LOOP, UnsupportedPathError, METHOD_NAME_ALL, emptyParams, checkOptionalParameter, splitPath, splitByStarRe
+- Simple note: Stores routes in array, checks each route linearly, returns first match, supports `*`, `*:param`, `:label{pat}`, `:label{pat}/`
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module export for RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter (index file)
+- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams
+- Simple note: Exports main RegExpRouter class and prepared router for Cloudflare Workers compatibility
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: RegExpRouter builds all matchers at startup (compile phase), uses Trie internally, matches via compiled regex
+- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, UnsupportedPathError, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, checkOptionalParameter, Matcher, ParamAssocArray, Node, ParamIndexMap, Trie, buildRegExp, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes, buildWildcardRegExp, findMiddleware, match
+- Simple note: Routes are preprocessed on add(), regex built once during match(), fast during request handling
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts
+- Purpose: Matcher interface for reg-exp-router, handles routing matching at request time
+- Names noticed: Matcher, ParamIndexMap, Result, emptyParam, METHOD_NAME_ALL, handlerMap, staticMap, match
+- Simple note: Builds matchers for each method on first request, checks staticMap first, falls back to regex match, caches matchers
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts
+- Purpose: TreeNode implementation for regexp-router, builds trie tree, constructs compiled regex from tree structure
+- Names noticed: Node, insert, buildRegExpStr, buildRegExp, Context, ParamAssocArray, PATH_ERROR, compareKey, Label, Wildcard, Literal, RegExp
+- Simple note: Recursively inserts path tokens into tree, constructs nested regex with parameters, ensures valid regex (no ambiguous patterns)
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts
+- Purpose: Trie implementation for regexp-router, converts path to tree structure and generates final compiled regex
+- Names noticed: Trie, insert, buildRegExp, Node, ParamAssocArray, Context, ReplacementMap, groups, labels, regex
+- Simple note: Replaces custom labels with placeholder markers during insert, generates final regex with all placeholders replaced at build time, ensures no duplicate param names
+
+## src/router/smart-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module export for SmartRouter (index file)
+- Names noticed: SmartRouter
+- Simple note: Simple re-export from 'router'
+
+## src/router/smart-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: SmartRouter intelligently wraps the most capable router needed for registered paths
+- Names noticed: SmartRouter, UnsupportedPathError, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, Router
+- Simple note: Tries each router in order, adds routes, uses first that doesn't throw, switches to just that router after all routes added
+
+## src/router/trie-router/index.ts
+- Purpose: Module export for TrieRouter (index file)
+- Names noticed: TrieRouter
+- Simple note: Simple re-export from 'router'
+
+## src/router/trie-router/router.ts
+- Purpose: TrieRouter implements tree-based path matching, stores routes in hierarchical tree structure
+- Names noticed: TrieRouter, insert, Node, add, match, search
+- Simple note: Builds tree of path tokens, walks tree matching request path, collects all matching handlers and params
+
+## src/router/trie-router/node.ts
+- Purpose: Tree node for trie router, stores method+handler sets at each node, matches by walking tree parts
+- Names noticed: Node, insert, search, HandlerParamsSet, HandlerSet, splitPath, splitRoutingPath, getPattern, match, optionalParameter
+- Simple note: Each node stores handlers for a method+path, traverses tree matching request parts, collects all handler+param matches, sorts by insertion order
+
+## src/router/common.case.test.ts
+- Purpose: Shared test utilities and common test cases for all router implementations
+- Names noticed: runTest, getSuiteHierarchy, describe, it, router, expect, skip, task
+- Simple note: Provides parametrized test runner accepting `skip` array and `newRouter` factory, defines common test cases like basic usage, reserved words, complex patterns, duplicate params
+
+## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Tests PatternRouter for common cases and router-specific behaviors
+- Names noticed: PatternRouter, expect, it, describe, runTest, beforeEach, after, skip
+- Simple note: Uses common cases test runner, skips UnsupportedPathError and trailing slash tests, has specific test for duplicate param name throwing error
+
+## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Tests LinearRouter for common cases and router-specific behaviors, skips unsupported features
+- Names noted: LinearRouter, expect, it, describe, runTest, beforeEach, after, skip, UnsupportedPathError
+- Simple note: Uses common cases test runner, skips params-per-handler-with-wildcard, captures with trailing wildcard, complex regexp, supports trailing slash with `/:id{d+}` patterns
+
+## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Tests RegExpRouter for common cases and unsupported path patterns
+- Names noted: RegExpRouter, expect, it, describe, runTest, beforeEach, after, skip, UnsupportedPathError, ParamStash
+- Simple note: Uses common cases test runner, skips duplicate param names, complex capturing groups, captures with trailing wildcard, static-after-dynamic conflicts, returns raw match structure
+
+## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Tests SmartRouter's dynamic router switching behavior
+- Names noted: SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, expect, it, describe, runTest, beforeEach, skip
+- Simple note: Runs common cases against SmartRouter with [RegExpRouter, TrieRouter](src/router/smart-router/router.ts:23), expects successful matching for all common cases
+
+## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts
+- Purpose: Tests TrieRouter for all common cases, fully compatible
+- Names noted: TrieRouter, expect, it, describe, runTest, beforeEach, skip
+- Simple note: Uses common cases test runner, no skip, passes all tests including complex patterns and edge cases
+
+## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts
+- Purpose: Unit tests for Node class in trie-router, tests tree insertion and search
+- Names noted: Node, insert, search, expect, it, describe, beforeEach, skip
+- Simple note: Tests root node behavior, wildcard handling, named params, regexp patterns, trailing slash, duplicate params, insertion ordering, complex multi-match, fallback behavior
+
+## Cross-File Checklist
+
+- **pattern-router**:
+  - Pattern-based routing with runtime regex compilation
+  - PatternRouter in src/router/pattern-router/router.ts
+  - Throws UnsupportedPathError for invalid paths like duplicate param names
+  - Trailing slashes allowed
+  - Suitable for small routes, unpredictable path changes
+
+- **linear-router**:
+  - Linear path matching with array-based storage
+  - LinearRouter in src/router/linear-router/router.ts
+  - Supports `*`, `:label`, `:label{pattern}`, `/{id}`
+  - Throws UnsupportedPathError for wildcard+param combination
+  - Fast on small route sets, scales linearly
+
+- **reg-exp-router**:
+  - Compile-time regex building with trie tree
+  - RegExpRouter in src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts
+  - Uses Node for tree, Trie for regex generation
+  - Compiled matcher returned for fast matches
+  - Throws UnsupportedPathError for ambiguous patterns
+  - Fast for most route sets, good regex efficiency
+
+- **smart-router**:
+  - Dynamic router wrapping (wraps first router that succeeds)
+  - SmartRouter in src/router/smart-router/router.ts
+  - Has configurable router list [RegExpRouter, TrieRouter]
+  - Switches to single active router after routes added
+  - Fast after initial wrap, supports all patterns
+
+- **trie-router**:
+  - Tree-based path matching with Node structure
+  - TrieRouter in src/router/trie-router/router.ts
+  - Uses Node class with multiple methods+handlers per path
+  - No UnsupportedPathError, accepts all valid paths
+  - Good balance of speed and flexibility
+
+## Test Files
+- **router/common.case.test.ts**: Shared test cases for all routers
+- **router/pattern-router/router.test.ts**: PatternRouter tests with skips
+- **router/linear-router/router.test.ts**: LinearRouter tests with skips
+- **router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts**: RegExpRouter tests with skips
+- **router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.test.ts**: PreparedRegExpRouter for serialization
+- **router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts**: Matcher implementation
+- **router/smart-router/router.test.ts**: SmartRouter integration tests
+- **router/trie-router/router.test.ts**: TrieRouter tests no skips
+- **router/trie-router/node.test.ts**: Node unit tests
\ No newline at end of file

parts.text:
"Benchmark task: routing-read-write-ledger
Repository: https://github.com/honojs/hono.git

Create a new file at bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md. This is a read/write endurance task, not a clever debugging task. First create the bench-artifacts directory if it does not exist. Then read every file in this exact list before writing the final document: src/hono.ts, src/hono-base.ts, src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/common.case.test.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts. The output file must be long but mechanically simple. Start with a title '# Routing Read/Write Ledger'. For each listed file, add a section whose heading is exactly '## <file path>'. Under each heading write three bullets: '- Purpose: ...', '- Names noticed: ...', and '- Simple note: ...'. Keep the notes plain and safe; do not change source code. After the per-file sections, add '## Cross-File Checklist' with one bullet per router family: pattern-router, linear-router, reg-exp-router, smart-router, trie-router. Include exact file paths in the final answer and report that only bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md was created.

Constraints:
- Do not ask the user to write prompts or inspect files manually.
- Prefer the smallest safe code change when edits are required.
- Include exact file paths, commands run, and verification status in the final answer."
